exercises.title: H2 vs Testcontainers — which bug is caught?
exercises.instructions:
Which of these bugs would H2 catch in @DataJpaTest? Which needs Testcontainers (real PostgreSQL)?
A: JPQL query has wrong field name (typo)
B: JSONB column type mismatch (VARCHAR vs JSONB)
C: Missing @NotNull JPA constraint
D: PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE syntax (upsert)
E: @Transactional rollback behavior on RuntimeException
F: PostgreSQL-specific RETURNING clause in native query
exercises.expected_solution:
H2 CATCHES (works with @DataJpaTest + H2):
  A: JPQL typo → both H2 and PostgreSQL throw 'unknown field' on @DataJpaTest startup
  C: @NotNull JPA constraint → Bean Validation runs on both H2 and PostgreSQL
  E: Transactional rollback → Spring behavior is the same regardless of DB

TESTCONTAINERS NEEDED (real PostgreSQL required):
  B: JSONB type mismatch → H2 silently accepts any string in JSONB column;
     PostgreSQL 15 enforces strict JSONB type (valid JSON only)
  D: ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE → PostgreSQL-specific syntax;
     H2 has different upsert syntax (MERGE INTO) — native query fails on H2
  F: RETURNING clause → PostgreSQL extension; H2 doesn't support it;
     @Query(nativeQuery=true) with RETURNING silently fails or errors on H2

RULE:
  H2: pure JPQL, JPA lifecycle, Spring transaction behavior
  Testcontainers: DB-specific SQL, custom types (JSONB, ARRAY), constraints, extensions

test_cases.expected:
H2 CATCHES (works with @DataJpaTest + H2):
  A: JPQL typo → both H2 and PostgreSQL throw 'unknown field' on @DataJpaTest startup
  C: @NotNull JPA constraint → Bean Validation runs on both H2 and PostgreSQL
  E: Transactional rollback → Spring behavior is the same regardless of DB

TESTCONTAINERS NEEDED (real PostgreSQL required):
  B: JSONB type mismatch → H2 silently accepts any string in JSONB column;
     PostgreSQL 15 enforces strict JSONB type (valid JSON only)
  D: ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE → PostgreSQL-specific syntax;
     H2 has different upsert syntax (MERGE INTO) — native query fails on H2
  F: RETURNING clause → PostgreSQL extension; H2 doesn't support it;
     @Query(nativeQuery=true) with RETURNING silently fails or errors on H2

RULE:
  H2: pure JPQL, JPA lifecycle, Spring transaction behavior
  Testcontainers: DB-specific SQL, custom types (JSONB, ARRAY), constraints, extensions

hints: H2: JPQL is DB-agnostic (Spring translates it)
ON CONFLICT: PostgreSQL-specific SQL → fails on H2
JSONB: H2 accepts any string; PostgreSQL validates JSON structure

exercises.title: Complete the Testcontainers setup
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks for a PostgreSQL Testcontainer.

blanks.hint: class-level annotation that manages container lifecycle
field-level annotation marking which containers to manage
overrides Spring properties at test startup with container URLs
method on PostgreSQLContainer that returns the JDBC connection URL

hints: @Testcontainers: tells JUnit 5 to manage container lifecycle
@Container static: shared container for all test methods
@DynamicPropertySource: static method that overrides Spring properties

exercises.title: First PostgreSQL Testcontainer
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete @SpringBootTest with PostgreSQLContainer:
1. @Testcontainers + @Container static PostgreSQLContainer
2. @DynamicPropertySource: url, username, password
3. @Autowired JdbcTemplate: execute a simple query
4. Test: SELECT 1 returns 1 (DB is alive and connected)
hints: static container: shared across all @Test methods in the class
getJdbcUrl(): returns jdbc:postgresql://localhost:{port}/rzp_test with dynamic port
SELECT 1: simplest possible SQL to verify DB connection is alive

exercises.title: PostgreSQL JSONB column test — catches H2 bug
exercises.instructions:
Prove Testcontainers catches JSONB type enforcement:
1. Payment entity with @Column(columnDefinition='jsonb') metadata field
2. PaymentRepository: save + find
3. Test: valid JSON saves OK
4. Test: invalid JSON string should fail with PSQLException
5. Show: H2 would silently accept invalid JSON
hints: @AutoConfigureTestDatabase(replace=NONE): don't replace with H2 (use the container)
JSONB: PostgreSQL validates JSON structure at insert time
H2 JSONB: stores as TEXT, no validation — bug hidden until production

exercises.title: Redis Testcontainer for caching tests
exercises.instructions:
Write RedisContainer integration test:
1. GenericContainer("redis:7-alpine").withExposedPorts(6379)
2. @DynamicPropertySource: spring.data.redis.host + port
3. RedisTemplate: set key-value + get
4. Test: TTL expiry (Thread.sleep for short TTL)
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): save+get works, TTL expires
hints: getMappedPort(6379): Testcontainers maps to a random host port (avoids conflicts)
GenericContainer: for any Docker image not natively supported by Testcontainers
TTL test: Thread.sleep + assertNull — tests real Redis expiry behavior (H2 cache can't do this)

exercises.title: Kafka Testcontainer for event-driven tests
exercises.instructions:
Write KafkaContainer integration test:
1. KafkaContainer(DockerImageName.parse('confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0'))
2. @DynamicPropertySource: spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers
3. KafkaTemplate: produce a PaymentEvent
4. @KafkaListener: consume it
5. @SpringBootTest: produce → consume within 5 seconds
hints: CountDownLatch: thread-safe mechanism to wait for async consumer
ConfluentKafkaContainer: Testcontainers wrapper for Confluent Kafka image
auto-offset-reset=earliest: consumer reads from beginning (important for test reliability)

exercises.title: Payment repository integration test with PostgreSQL
exercises.instructions:
Write comprehensive PaymentRepository integration test:
1. PostgreSQLContainer
2. Payment entity: id, merchantId, amount (BigDecimal), status, createdAt
3. Save, findByMerchantId, findByStatus queries
4. Test: amount precision maintained in PostgreSQL (BigDecimal scale)
5. @DataJpaTest + @Testcontainers (3 tests): save, find by merchant, amount precision
hints: @AutoConfigureTestDatabase(replace=NONE): critical — stops Spring from replacing with H2
DECIMAL(12,2): PostgreSQL maintains exact precision for financial amounts
compareTo(0): BigDecimal equality — .equals() also checks scale

exercises.title: Container reuse across test classes
exercises.instructions:
Demonstrate container reuse pattern:
1. Shared PostgreSQL container in abstract base class
2. Two test classes extend the base (share same container)
3. @BeforeEach truncates tables (don't restart container!)
4. @SpringBootTest: both test classes use same container instance
hints: Static field in abstract class: shared across all subclasses (same JVM instance)
@BeforeEach TRUNCATE: clean state without costly container restart
withReuse(true): Testcontainers keeps container alive between test runs (local dev speed)

exercises.title: Full payment flow integration test
exercises.instructions:
Write full Razorpay payment flow integration test:
1. PostgreSQL + Redis containers
2. PaymentService: creates payment, caches result in Redis
3. Test: create payment → saved in DB → cached in Redis → cache hit on second read
4. Test: payment status update → cache invalidated
5. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): cache works, invalidation works
hints: flushAll(): clear Redis between tests (test isolation)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS: idempotent schema creation in @BeforeEach
Two containers: both declared static for shared lifecycle

exercises.title: Testcontainers lifecycle analysis
exercises.expected_solution:
1. Non-static `postgres` container startups:
   5 startups — one per @Test method
   Lifecycle: @BeforeEach → start container; @AfterEach → stop container
   5 tests × 45s startup = 225 seconds just in container overhead

2. Static `shared` container startups:
   1 startup — once for the entire test class
   Lifecycle: @BeforeAll → start; @AfterAll → stop
   All 5 tests share the same PostgreSQL instance
   Test time: 45s startup + actual test execution time

3. Static is much faster:
   Non-static: 225s overhead (5 × 45s)
   Static: 45s overhead (1 × 45s)
   Speedup: 5x for this class, grows linearly with test count

4. Use non-static @Container when:
   - Each test needs a COMPLETELY FRESH database state (not just truncated)
   - Tests modify shared DB state and cleanup is too complex
   - Isolated connection pools (rare)
   - Most cases: use static + @BeforeEach TRUNCATE (faster and clean)

5. TestClass B with static PostgreSQLContainer:
   - A NEW separate container starts for TestClass B
   - Not shared with TestClass A's static container
   - Two PostgreSQL containers running simultaneously during the test run
   - Solution for sharing: abstract base class with static container field
     OR Testcontainers' singleton pattern (withReuse=true + static block)

test_cases.expected:
1. Non-static `postgres` container startups:
   5 startups — one per @Test method
   Lifecycle: @BeforeEach → start container; @AfterEach → stop container
   5 tests × 45s startup = 225 seconds just in container overhead

2. Static `shared` container startups:
   1 startup — once for the entire test class
   Lifecycle: @BeforeAll → start; @AfterAll → stop
   All 5 tests share the same PostgreSQL instance
   Test time: 45s startup + actual test execution time

3. Static is much faster:
   Non-static: 225s overhead (5 × 45s)
   Static: 45s overhead (1 × 45s)
   Speedup: 5x for this class, grows linearly with test count

4. Use non-static @Container when:
   - Each test needs a COMPLETELY FRESH database state (not just truncated)
   - Tests modify shared DB state and cleanup is too complex
   - Isolated connection pools (rare)
   - Most cases: use static + @BeforeEach TRUNCATE (faster and clean)

5. TestClass B with static PostgreSQLContainer:
   - A NEW separate container starts for TestClass B
   - Not shared with TestClass A's static container
   - Two PostgreSQL containers running simultaneously during the test run
   - Solution for sharing: abstract base class with static container field
     OR Testcontainers' singleton pattern (withReuse=true + static block)

hints: Non-static: per-method lifecycle (slow)
Static: per-class lifecycle (fast)
Abstract base class: shares static container across subclasses

exercises.title: Complete payment repository suite with PostgreSQL
exercises.instructions:
Build complete payment test suite:
1. PostgreSQLContainer (static, shared)
2. Payment entity: id, merchantId, amount, status, gateway, createdAt
3. PaymentRepository with 3 custom queries: findByMerchantId, findByStatus, sumByMerchantId
4. @BeforeEach: truncate for test isolation
5. @DataJpaTest + @Testcontainers (4 tests): save, findByMerchant, status filter, sum
hints: deleteAll() in @BeforeEach: clean state between tests
SUM JPQL: returns null if no rows (be careful with compareTo)
BigDecimal compareTo(0): checks equality ignoring scale

exercises.title: Kafka end-to-end event test
exercises.instructions:
Test complete Kafka event pipeline:
1. KafkaContainer
2. PaymentEventProducer: sends PaymentCreatedEvent to 'payment.created'
3. PaymentEventConsumer: @KafkaListener updates in-memory status map
4. Test: produce event → consumer updates status within 10 seconds
5. @SpringBootTest (1 test): full round-trip verified
hints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe for cross-thread communication in tests
CountDownLatch.await(10, SECONDS): test-safe waiting for async consumer
reset latch in @Test: CountDownLatch can't be reset — create new one

exercises.title: Complete Razorpay payment integration suite
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete Razorpay integration test suite:
1. PostgreSQL + Redis containers (static, shared)
2. Payment entity + repository (save, findByMerchantId, sumByMerchant)
3. Redis caching: cache payment on save, invalidate on status update
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): CRUD, merchant query, cache hit, cache invalidation
5. All 4 pass
hints: flushAll(): use connection().flushAll() for StringRedisTemplate
deleteAll() in @BeforeEach: clean JPA state between tests
Two static containers: both managed by @Testcontainers lifecycle

exercises.title: GitHub Actions CI configuration for Testcontainers
exercises.instructions:
Write GitHub Actions workflow for Testcontainers CI:
1. .github/workflows/integration-tests.yml
2. Docker socket available (built-in to ubuntu-latest)
3. Maven test phase with integration tests enabled
4. Cache: Docker layer cache + Maven deps
5. @SpringBootTest: verify test runs correctly in documented CI environment
exercises.expected_solution:
# .github/workflows/integration-tests.yml
name: Integration Tests (Testcontainers)

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  integration-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Docker daemon is pre-installed on ubuntu-latest — no extra setup needed!
    # Testcontainers uses /var/run/docker.sock automatically
    
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    
    - name: Set up JDK 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: '21'
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
    
    - name: Cache Docker images (Testcontainers)
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
    
    - name: Run integration tests
      run: mvn verify -P integration-tests
      env:
        # Testcontainers reads this to find Docker:
        TESTCONTAINERS_HOST_OVERRIDE: localhost
        # Optional: use Testcontainers Cloud for faster CI:
        # TC_CLOUD_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.TC_CLOUD_TOKEN }}
    
    - name: Upload test results
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: test-results
        path: target/surefire-reports/

# pom.xml profile for integration tests:
# <profiles>
#   <profile>
#     <id>integration-tests</id>
#     <build><plugins><plugin>
#       <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
#       <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
#       <executions><execution>
#         <goals><goal>integration-test</goal><goal>verify</goal></goals>
#       </execution></executions>
#     </plugin></plugins></build>
#   </profile>
# </profiles>

# WHY ubuntu-latest:
# Docker daemon included — no extra setup
# Testcontainers auto-detects Docker socket
# Faster than self-hosted runners for most builds
# Alternative: Testcontainers Cloud (remote containers, even faster)

public class CIDoc { public static void main(String[] a){
    System.out.println("GitHub Actions + Testcontainers: Docker built-in, zero config");
} }
hints: ubuntu-latest: Docker pre-installed (most common CI Testcontainers setup)
TESTCONTAINERS_HOST_OVERRIDE: fixes 'host not reachable' on some CI systems
Testcontainers Cloud: remote containers, no local Docker needed (paid)

exercises.title: Complete M25 T1 Testcontainers synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M25 T1 synthesis:
1. PostgreSQL container (static)
2. Redis container (static)
3. Entity + repository (save, find)
4. Redis set + get + TTL
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): DB save, Redis cache, TTL expiry, both containers alive
6. All 4 pass
hints: isRunning(): verify both containers are alive (basic health check)
TTL test: 1s TTL + 2s sleep = expired (fast enough for tests)
flushAll() + deleteAll(): clean both Redis and PostgreSQL between tests
